dimension_averages_note: brand_voice average is calculated from 3 scored articles only (items 85, 86, 88); items 83 and 87 returned UNKNOWN brand verdicts and are excluded from this average

recurring_issues.pattern: Zero named citations — 'research shows' and 'research demonstrates' used without attribution across all articles
recurring_issues.impact: Suppresses Proof (P) scores to near-floor across every article; AI systems cannot cite unattributed assertions; kills AI Citability and E-E-A-T simultaneously

recurring_issues.pattern: No canonical direct-answer block in first 150 words — both QAPEDS gates fail on every article
recurring_issues.impact: AI systems cannot extract a concise, citable answer; AIO snippet, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini all require an isolatable direct-answer block to surface the content; 0% platform readiness is a direct consequence

recurring_issues.pattern: No first-person practitioner signals — no named teachers, schools, 'we tested' framing, or Wayground platform data cited
recurring_issues.impact: Experience sub-component of E-E-A-T scores near zero; content reads as generic AI-generated text, reducing Google quality evaluator confidence and suppressing Share of Voice

recurring_issues.pattern: Statistical density near zero — no percentages, effect sizes, or data points with attribution and date
recurring_issues.impact: Statistical signals are +33% GEO citability lift per research; absence across most articles means the cluster cannot compete for AI-cited placements on data-seeking queries

cluster_recommendations.issue: Cluster-wide citation and proof failure — all 5 articles use unattributed 'research shows' phrasing
cluster_recommendations.fix: Replace every instance of 'research shows,' 'studies suggest,' and 'research demonstrates' with a named citation in 'According to [Source, Year]' format. Minimum 3 named sources per article. Recommended sources: Fredricks, Blumenfeld & Paris (2004) on multidimensional engagement; Hattie (2009) Visible Learning on effect sizes; Gallup Student Poll (2023) for disengagement statistics; Deci & Ryan Self-Determination Theory for motivation framing; NWEA 2023 MAP Growth norms for achievement data.

cluster_recommendations.issue: No direct-answer blocks — both QAPEDS gates fail on every article, resulting in 0% platform readiness
cluster_recommendations.fix: Rewrite the opening of each article to include a 1-3 sentence canonical answer (40-60 words) within the first 150 words. The answer must be structurally isolatable — a standalone paragraph, not buried in narrative. For example, item 86 ('student motivation and engagement') should open with a definition of both terms and their relationship before any contextual framing. Each article's first H2 opening paragraph must also be trimmed to under 40 words to qualify for AEO extraction.

cluster_recommendations.issue: No FAQ-structured sections — Perplexity readiness is 0% and FAQPage schema cannot be implemented
cluster_recommendations.fix: Add a dedicated FAQ section (H2: 'Frequently asked questions') to each article with 4-5 Q&A pairs using H3 question headings followed by 40-60 word answers. For the engagement cluster, natural FAQ candidates include: 'What is the difference between motivation and engagement?', 'How do you measure student engagement?', 'What causes student disengagement?' This section enables FAQPage schema on all 5 articles and directly unlocks Perplexity citation readiness.

cluster_recommendations.issue: No practitioner voice — content reads as generic and lacks E-E-A-T Experience signals
cluster_recommendations.fix: Add at least one named practitioner scenario per article: a specific teacher role, grade level, subject, and outcome. Format: 'A 7th grade science teacher using Wayground found that...' or 'Based on [X] teachers in the Wayground network...' Where Wayground platform data exists (usage stats, activity completion rates, teacher feedback), cite it with 'Based on X users' framing. This is the single fastest lever to differentiate the cluster from competing generic content.

cluster_recommendations.issue: Brand compliance is critical — 3 of 3 scored articles returned FAIL; 2 have unknown brand verdicts pending review
cluster_recommendations.fix: Priority brand fixes across the cluster: (1) Convert all H2 and H3 headings from title case to sentence case — item 87 has this documented explicitly and it is likely widespread. (2) Audit for em dashes in body copy — zero tolerance per brand rules. (3) Check for 'quiz tool,' 'quiz app,' or 'Quizizz' references and replace with 'Wayground' or 'supplemental learning platform.' Items 83 and 87 must be submitted for brand review immediately to resolve UNKNOWN verdicts before publication.

<output>

# Engagement Cluster — Synthesis Report

## Cluster overview

The Engagement cluster is the lowest-performing group evaluated to date, with an average GEO composite of **44.9** and an average QAPEDS of **4.28** — both well below publishable thresholds. Every article in the cluster fails every AI platform readiness check (0% AIO, ChatGPT, and Perplexity ready), and brand compliance is critical, with 3 of 3 scored articles returning FAIL verdicts and 2 articles still awaiting brand review. The cluster's core structural problem is uniform: all five articles fail the same two QAPEDS gates simultaneously, meaning no article is currently citable by any AI system.

---

## Score summary

| Dimension | Cluster Avg | Target (70+) | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| AI Citability | 45.8 | 70 | -24.2 |
| E-E-A-T | 39.6 | 70 | -30.4 |
| Platform Optimization | 38.6 | 70 | -31.4 |
| Brand Voice* | 28.7 | 70 | -41.3 |
| SEO Structure | 49.8 | 70 | -20.2 |
| Schema Recommendations | 75.4 | 70 | **+5.4** |
| **GEO Composite** | **44.9** | **70** | **-25.1** |
| **QAPEDS** | **4.28/10** | **7.0+** | **-2.72** |

*Brand Voice average calculated from 3 scored articles only (items 85, 86, 88). Items 83 and 87 have UNKNOWN brand verdicts pending review.

Schema Recommendations is the lone above-target dimension — the articles have structure that could support schema, but the content quality issues must be resolved before schema implementation would have meaningful impact. Every other dimension is below the 70-point threshold, with Brand Voice and Platform Optimization as the most severe gaps.

---

## Top 3 recurring issues

### 1. Zero named citations — affects all 5 articles

Every article in the cluster uses "research shows," "research demonstrates," or "studies suggest" without a single named source. This is not a minor citation style issue — it is a structural E-E-A-T failure. Under the rubric, "research shows" without a named source caps the Evidence Presence sub-score at floor level. It also directly suppresses AI Citability, because AI systems evaluating content for citation prioritize factual density with attribution. Per GEO research, named citations are a +28% citability signal and statistics with attribution are +33%. The Engagement cluster currently captures neither. This single pattern is responsible for the majority of the E-E-A-T gap across the cluster.

### 2. No direct-answer blocks in the first 150 words — affects all 5 articles

Both QAPEDS hard gates fail on every article. Gate 1 (Explicit Question) and Gate 2 (Direct Answer) require a clear interrogative question and a 1-3 sentence canonical answer within the first 150 words. Instead, each article opens with narrative framing, rhetorical questions, or problem scenarios before pivoting to content. This means AI systems — Google AIO, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Bing Copilot — have no extractable answer to surface. The 0% platform readiness across all five articles is a direct downstream consequence of this structural pattern. This is the single highest-leverage fix available: rewriting five article openings would unlock platform readiness across the entire cluster.

### 3. No practitioner signals or first-person experience — affects 4 of 5 articles

The Experience sub-component of E-E-A-T requires first-hand evidence: named teachers, specific classroom scenarios, Wayground platform data, or "we tested" framing. None of these signals appear in four of the five articles. Classroom examples exist in some articles (noted in item 85's evaluation), but they are presented as illustrative constructs rather than attributed real-world experiences. Google's quality evaluator framework explicitly differentiates between content that demonstrates experience and content that describes scenarios. Without practitioner voice, the cluster reads as generic AI-generated text — which suppresses quality ratings and reduces Share of Voice against competitor content that does carry named teacher or school references.

---

## Platform readiness

| Platform | Ready | % |
|---|---|---|
| Google AIO | 0 of 5 | 0% |
| ChatGPT | 0 of 5 | 0% |
| Perplexity | 0 of 5 | 0% |
| Gemini | 0 of 5 | 0% |
| Bing Copilot | 0 of 5 | 0% |

The Engagement cluster has a 0% platform readiness rate — the worst possible outcome. No article in the cluster is currently in a position to be cited by any major AI system. The cause is consistent across all five articles: the absence of a direct-answer block (which blocks AIO and ChatGPT readiness) and the absence of FAQ-structured sections with factual density (which blocks Perplexity readiness). These are fixable structural problems, not content quality problems that require fundamental rewrites. Implementing the direct-answer block and FAQ section recommendations in Cluster Recommendations 2 and 3 below would lift this metric substantially across all five articles.

---

## Brand compliance

| Verdict | Count |
|---|---|
| PASS | 0 |
| WARN | 0 |
| FAIL | 3 |
| UNKNOWN | 2 |
